parts.text:
This table compares the three most common structures for U.S. small businesses. Note that an **S-Corp** is a tax designation, not a distinct legal entity; both LLCs and Corporations can elect S-Corp status with the IRS.

### U.S. Business Entity Comparison Table

| Feature | Limited Liability Company (LLC) | S-Corporation (Tax Election) | C-Corporation |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Taxation Model** | Pass-through (Default) | Pass-through | Double Taxation (Entity + Dividend) |
| **Federal Income Tax** | Owners pay on personal returns | Owners pay on personal returns | Entity pays 21% flat; Owners pay on dividends |
| **Self-Employment Tax** | Paid on 100% of net profits | Paid only on "reasonable salary" | None (but FICA on wages) |
| **Ownership Limits** | Unlimited (Individuals, Corps, Trusts) | Max 100 (U.S. citizens/residents only) | Unlimited (Global, Venture Capital) |
| **Classes of Stock** | Flexible/N/A | One class of stock only | Multiple classes (Common/Preferred) |
| **Governing Document** | Operating Agreement | Bylaws | Bylaws |
| **Annual Formalities** | Minimal (Annual report, fee) | High (Board meetings, minutes, payroll) | High (Board meetings, minutes, payroll) |
| **Loss Deduction** | Owners deduct losses on personal returns | Owners deduct losses (subject to basis) | Losses remain at corporate level |
| **Fringe Benefits** | Taxable to 2%+ owners | Taxable to 2%+ owners | Deductible by corp, tax-free to owner |
| **Entity Longevity** | Can be dissolved by owner exit | Perpetual | Perpetual |
| **VC/IPO Potential** | Very Low | Low | High (Standard for investors) |

---

### Quick Decision Matrix

| Choose an **LLC** if... | Choose an **S-Corp** if... | Choose a **C-Corp** if... |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| You want maximum simplicity and minimal paperwork. | You earn enough profit to save on self-employment taxes (usually $60k+). | You plan to seek Venture Capital or go public. |
| You have foreign or corporate owners/partners. | You are a U.S. citizen/resident looking for a "middle ground" tax strategy. | You want to reinvest all profits back into the company. |
| You want flexible profit-sharing (not tied to ownership %). | You want to pay yourself a formal salary + distributions. | You want to offer complex stock options to employees. |
| You are a solo freelancer or a small local partnership. | You want to minimize the 15.3% SE tax on business growth. | You want the absolute strongest "corporate veil" protection. |

***Disclaimer:** This table is for informational purposes and does not constitute legal or tax advice. Consult with a CPA or attorney before filing.*
